description: Create a content slide on Chapter 9 with a two-column layout: explanatory text on the left and a horizontal bar chart on the right showing risk management operating model statements and percentages.

header.headline: Chapter 9
header.sub: In terms of reporting lines, responsibilities and interactions with different functions, our results show the expanding remit of CROs and their increased involvement across the business and with different levels of the organization.

left.body: In terms of reporting lines, responsibilities and interactions with different functions, our results show the expanding remit of CROs and their increased involvement across the business and with different levels of the organization. A large majority (72%) of CROs have a direct relationship with the board or a board-level risk committee and nearly as many (65%) report directly to the CEO, with about a quarter (27%) reporting to the CFO.

right.title: Figure 20: Please select the statements that best describe the risk management operating model within your organization, focusing on reporting lines, responsibilities and interactions among different functions.

series.label: The CRO has an independent reporting relationship to the board or a risk committee of the board
The second-line risk management functions assists and/or facilitates first-line business in completing risk identification and assessment processes
The CRO reports reports directly to the CEO
IA’s findings are regularly viewed and discussed with the second-line risk management function
We have a well-defined shared reliance model between internal audit (IA) and the second-line risk function
IA and the risk management function coordinate audits and independent second-line testing engagements to minimize impact to the business
The first line provides reporting on their risks to the second-line risk management function
The second-line risk management function interacts with first-line business stakeholders mostly in an independent oversight and challenge capacity
Escalation protocols and communication channels between the first and second lines of defense are well-defined

footer.source: Second Annual EY/EIU Global Insurance Risk Management Survey
